\documentclass[11pt]{article}
\usepackage[margin=1in]{geometry}
\usepackage{array,booktabs,makecell,multirow,tabularx,longtable,colortbl,xcolor,graphicx}
\usepackage{arydshln}
\usepackage[T1]{fontenc}
\usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
\title{Simple Tables}
\author{Source-backed grouped table sample}
\date{}
\begin{document}
\maketitle
\section{Tables}
These tables are grouped from source-backed LaTeX table data and compiled as a single benchmark data point.

\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Source table 1: 2512.03307\_table\_5}
\resizebox{\linewidth}{!}{%
\begin{tabular}{|c|cccccccccc|}
\hline
max-min iter. & $\mu_l$ & $\mu_h$ & $\mu_{x_{in}}$ & $\mu_{z_x}$ & $\mu_{r_{s}}$ & $\mu_c$ & $\mu_a$ & $\mu_{r_{cat}}$ & $\mu_{m}$ & $\mu_d$ \\
\hline
1  & 5 & 10 & 3 & 5 & 1.0 & 8 & tanh & 0.9 & 0.4 & uniform \\
2  & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.1 & 2 & identity & 1.0 & 0.4 & uniform \\
3  & 12 & 128 & 13 & 200 & 0.0 & 2 & identity & 0.4 & 0.0 & uniform \\
4  & 3 & 5 & 2 & 6 & 0.6 & 2 & tanh & 1.0 & 0.0 & exponential \\
5  & 10 & 64 & 8 & 32 & 0.4 & 2 & tanh & 1.0 & 0.0 & uniform \\
6  & 8 & 32 & 5 & 13 & 0.8 & 10 & identity & 0.0 & 0.2 & normal \\
7  & 10 & 64 & 8 & 162 & 0.9 & 6 & elu & 0.6 & 0.0 & normal \\
8  & 5 & 10 & 3 & 13 & 0.0 & 10 & tanh & 0.8 & 0.0 & uniform \\
9  & 5 & 10 & 3 & 5 & 0.3 & 6 & identity & 0.6 & 0.0 & exponential \\
10 & 5 & 10 & 3 & 5 & 0.1 & 10 & tanh & 0.5 & 0.0 & normal \\
11 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.0 & 8 & relu & 1.0 & 0.0 & normal \\
12 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.6 & 4 & relu & 0.6 & 0.0 & exponential \\
13 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.4 & 6 & tanh & 0.6 & 0.0 & normal \\
14 & 5 & 10 & 3 & 5 & 0.8 & 8 & identity & 0.8 & 0.0 & exponential \\
15 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 1.0 & 8 & elu & 0.6 & 0.0 & uniform \\
16 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 6 & 0.7 & 6 & identity & 0.3 & 0.0 & uniform \\
17 & 8 & 32 & 5 & 91 & 0.0 & 2 & tanh & 1.0 & 0.0 & exponential \\
18 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.0 & 8 & tanh & 1.0 & 0.0 & exponential \\
19 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 6 & 0.2 & 6 & tanh & 0.2 & 0.0 & exponential \\
20 & 12 & 128 & 13 & 200 & 0.2 & 2 & identity & 0.1 & 0.0 & exponential \\

22 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.3 & 8 & identity & 0.2 & 0.0 & uniform \\
23 & 5 & 10 & 3 & 5 & 0.2 & 4 & tanh & 0.3 & 0.0 & normal \\
24 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.8 & 8 & identity & 0.1 & 0.0 & uniform \\
25 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.2 & 4 & relu & 1.0 & 0.0 & exponential \\
26 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.5 & 8 & tanh & 0.8 & 0.0 & normal \\
27 & 8 & 32 & 5 & 13 & 0.1 & 4 & identity & 0.0 & 0.0 & exponential \\
28 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 6 & 0.4 & 4 & tanh & 0.1 & 0.0 & exponential \\
29 & 3 & 5 & 2 & 5 & 0.1 & 8 & identity & 0.2 & 0.0 & exponential \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
}
\end{table}


\begin{table}[htbp]
\centering
\caption{Source table 2: 2512.02195\_table\_6}
\resizebox{\linewidth}{!}{%
\begin{tabular}[t]{@{}llll@{}}
\hline
\# & Word type & $f$ \\ \hline
1. & \textit{te} (‘at’, ‘to’, \textsc{comp}) & 1018 \\
2. & \textit{dat} (‘that’) & 658 \\
3. & \textit{niet} (‘not’) & 650 \\

13. & \textit{alle} (‘all’) & 215 \\
14. & \textit{een} (‘a’, ‘one’) & 212 \\
15. & \textit{ik} (‘I’) & 194 \\
16. & \textit{massa's} (‘masses’) & 186 \\
17. & \textit{het} (‘the’, \textsc{neut:sg}) & 180 \\
18. & \textit{op} (‘on’) & 178 \\
19. & \textit{deze} (‘this’, \textsc{masc/fem}) & 172 \\
20. & \textit{weinig} (‘a few’) & 171 \\
21. & \textit{daar} (‘there’) & 163 \\
22. & \textit{aan} (‘to’, \textsc{prep}) & 161 \\
23. & \textit{veel} (‘a lot of’) & 155 \\
24. & \textit{hier} (‘here’) & 146 \\
25. & \textit{voor} (‘for’) & 144 \\
26. & \textit{dit} (‘this’, \textsc{neut}) & 140 \\
27. & \textit{zijn} (‘are’, ‘be’, ‘his’) & 130 \\
28. & \textit{door} (‘by’) & 122 \\
29. & \textit{in} (‘in’) & 120 \\
30. & \textit{menig} (‘many’, \textsc{sg}) & 118 \\
31. & \textit{menige} (‘many’, \textsc{pl}) & 111 \\
32. & \textit{je} (‘you’, \textsc{inform}) & 110 \\
33. & \textit{die} (‘that’, \textsc{masc/fem}) & 103 \\
34. & \textit{waar} (‘where’) & 97 \\
35. & \textit{sommige} (‘some’, \textsc{pl}) & 90 \\ \hline
\end{tabular}
}
\end{table}

\end{document}

#align(center)[
  #text(size: 10pt)[Table 1: Source table 1: 2512.03307\_table\_5]
  #v(4pt)
  #set text(size: 8pt)
  #table(
    columns: 12,
    stroke: none,
    align: center,
    inset: (x: 3pt, y: 2pt),
    table.hline(),
    [max-min iter.], [$mu_l$], [$mu_h$], [$mu_(x_(i n))$], [$mu_(z_x)$], [$mu_(r_s)$], [$mu_c$], [$mu_a$], [$mu_(r_(c a t))$], [$mu_m$], [$mu_d$], [],
    table.hline(),
    ..t1_rows.map(r => (r.at(0), r.at(1), r.at(2), r.at(3), r.at(4), r.at(5), r.at(6), r.at(7), r.at(8), r.at(9), r.at(10), [])).flatten(),
    table.hline(),
  )
]

  #set text(size: 7pt)
  #table(
    columns: 11,
    stroke: none,
    align: center,
    inset: (x: 3pt, y: 1.2pt),
    table.hline(),
    [max-min iter.], [$mu_l$], [$mu_h$], [$mu_(x_(i n))$], [$mu_(z_x)$], [$mu_(r_s)$], [$mu_c$], [$mu_a$], [$mu_(r_(c a t))$], [$mu_m$], [$mu_d$],
    table.hline(),
    ..t1_rows.flatten(),
    table.hline(),
  )
]

python3 -c "
import re
with open('output.typ','r') as f:
    s = f.read()
# Replace backtick followed by any text with unicode left single quote
s = s.replace('\`', '\u2018')
with open('output.typ','w') as f:
    f.write(s)
print('done')
"
